Article_Title: Optimizing HMI for Intelligent Electric Vehicles Using BCI and Deep Neural Networks with Genetic Algorithms
Reference_Text_Preview: The data collection procedure involved participants wearing the Mindwave EEG device and starting the...
Full_Reference_Text: The data collection procedure involved participants wearing the Mindwave EEG device and starting the data collection app on a PC. The collected data included EEG raw data and five sub-frequency types: Delta, Theta, Alpha, Beta, Gamma, and e-Sense. The experimental setup defined specific parameters for EEG data collection: a sampling rate of 512 Hz to ensure high-resolution data capture. The headset displayed the virtual driving environment, overlaying the AR HMI onto the physical control screens of the EV.

Article_Title: Optimizing HMI for Intelligent Electric Vehicles Using BCI and Deep Neural Networks with Genetic Algorithms
Reference_Text_Preview: This research explores the interaction design of the main control human-machine interaction interfac...
Full_Reference_Text: This research explores the interaction design of the main control human-machine interaction interfaces (HMIs) for intelligent electric vehicles (EVs) by integrating neural network predictions with genetic algorithm optimizations. This study analyzed primary HMIs for intelligent electric vehicles (EVs) using established guidelines, industry standards, and the Electric Vehicle UI Design Specification. The 15 items were categorized into five factors: Factor 1: Layout mode, Factor 2: Interactive mode, Factor 3: Color mode, Factor 4: Feedback mode, Factor 5: Prompt model.

Article_Title: Optimizing HMI for Intelligent Electric Vehicles Using BCI and Deep Neural Networks with Genetic Algorithms
Reference_Text_Preview: This iterative process identified the most effective HMI configurations. The GA applied evolutionary...
Full_Reference_Text: This iterative process identified the most effective HMI configurations. The GA applied evolutionary processes to a population of HMI designs, with fitness evaluated based on DNN predictions. After 185 iterations, the optimal individual was identified with a preference score of approximately 5.570. The simulation involved driving on a straight, two-lane city road for 5 min at a speed of 30 km/h. Participants included 32 university students (18 males, 14 females) aged 19 to 25, all specializing in product design.

Article_Title: Optimizing HMI for Intelligent Electric Vehicles Using BCI and Deep Neural Networks with Genetic Algorithms
Reference_Text_Preview: This study utilizes a brain—computer interface (BCI)—based deep neural network (DNN) and genetic alg...
Full_Reference_Text: This study utilizes a brain—computer interface (BCI)—based deep neural network (DNN) and genetic algorithm (GA) method. The deep neural network (DNN) topology linking HMI visual coding to e-Sense prediction comprises three layers: an input layer with 26 neurons, three fully connected hidden layers, and an output layer with one neurone. The network utilized rectified linear unit (ReLU) activation functions across the hidden layers, with a regression layer at the output. Deep neural networks (DNNs) play a key role in the personalized human-machine interaction interfaces (HMIs) of intelligent electric vehicles. By analyzing complex user data, interactions are customized, the learning environment is optimized, and the user experience is enhanced.

Article_Title: Optimizing HMI for Intelligent Electric Vehicles Using BCI and Deep Neural Networks with Genetic Algorithms
Reference_Text_Preview: This research identified essential HMI elements for intelligent electric vehicles to understand user...
Full_Reference_Text: This research identified essential HMI elements for intelligent electric vehicles to understand user interaction needs. The developed DNN-GA analyzed user behavior patterns from EEG data to predict preferences and attention levels during driving. These predictions guided the GA in optimizing HMI layouts. In this experiment, EEG data were measured using an AR system integrated into a real vehicle cockpit environment, providing an immersive and realistic driving experience. Participants sequentially interacted with the AR HMI prototype's main control interface, performing tasks such as adjusting the rearview mirror, turning on driving lights, adjusting the seat, closing the sunshade, activating the 360° driving camera, turning on music, and, finally, opening the map. This setup ensured a realistic and interactive evaluation of the HMI design.

Article_Title: Exploiting Generative Models for Performance Predictions of 3D Car Designs
Reference_Text_Preview: Geometric deep-generative models, like autoencoders (AEs)[1] and variational autoencoders (VAEs) [2]...
Full_Reference_Text: Geometric deep-generative models, like autoencoders (AEs)[1] and variational autoencoders (VAEs) [2] learn low-dimensional latent representations of 3D geometric models in an unsupervised fashion, which allows to efficiently explore the learned design features and generate novel realistic 3D designs [3], [4]. Furthermore, the latent space of autoencoders has also been utilized for many predictive tasks in the chemistry and biology domain[5], allowing the assessment of the chemical compounds before committing to an expensive synthesis.

Reference_Text_Preview: In automotive digital development, engineers utilize multiple virtual prototyping tools to design an...
Full_Reference_Text: In automotive digital development, engineers utilize multiple virtual prototyping tools to design and assess the performance of 3D shapes. However, accurate performance simulations are computationally expensive and time-consuming, which may be prohibitive for design optimization tasks. To address this challenge, we envision a 3D design assistance system for design exploration with performance assessment in the automotive domain.

Article_Title: Exploiting Generative Models for Performance Predictions of 3D Car Designs
Reference_Text_Preview: we use machine learning-based surrogate models to predict the performances of car shapes based on th...
Full_Reference_Text: we use machine learning-based surrogate models to predict the performances of car shapes based on the low-dimensional representation learned by 3D point cloud (variational) autoencoders. Furthermore, we exploit the stochastic nature of the representation learned by variational autoencoders to augment the training data for our surrogate models. We utilize the regularized latent space of a variational point cloud autoencoder for fast design prototyping and data augmentation.

Reference_Text_Preview: In modern industry and smart manufacturing, industries are being reshaped by two forces: the power o...
Full_Reference_Text: In modern industry and smart manufacturing, industries are being reshaped by two forces: the power of crowds and artificial intelligence (AI) represented by large language models (LLMs). Under these two forces, the fields of engineering design and product innovation design are rapidly developing along two paths: crowd intelligence innovation (CII) represented by the Problem–Solution (P-S) Co-evolution Model, and AI-Generated Content (AIGC), which utilizes AI technology to generate content.

Reference_Text_Preview: Knowledge-based design (KBD) combined with design cognition (DC) mostly utilizes data sources in the...
Full_Reference_Text: Knowledge-based design (KBD) combined with design cognition (DC) mostly utilizes data sources in the form of natural language text. In this study, Chinese invention patents are selected as the knowledge source, and the relevant data can be downloaded from the Patent Data Service Pilot System of the State Intellectual Property Office. In this study, the full text of patent text is utilized to train the Skip-gram model so as to obtain the contextual semantic vectors of all the relevant knowledge elements.

Article_Title: The Generative Generic-Field Design Method Based on Design Cognition and Knowledge Reasoning
Reference_Text_Preview: Based on the topic model, a statistical model widely used in semantic analysis and text mining, Conc...
Full_Reference_Text: Based on the topic model, a statistical model widely used in semantic analysis and text mining, ConceptTM, is proposed to generate a set of physical structures containing implicit connections for a given technical field and functional requirements. ConceptTM adopts the PGM, especially the infrastructure of the topic model, to systematically express the complex and implicit correlations among physical structures. The modeling thinking of the probabilistic topic model is to regard the text as the probability distribution of the topic; that is, each text is composed of several topics, and the topic is regarded as the probability distribution of words.

Reference_Text_Preview: This study proposes a method based on design cognition and knowledge reasoning. Firstly, through the...
Full_Reference_Text: This study proposes a method based on design cognition and knowledge reasoning. Firstly, through the problem formulation, a generative universal domain design framework and knowledge base are constructed. Secondly, the knowledge-based discrete physical structure set generation method and system architecture generation method are proposed. Finally, the application tool Intelligent Design Assistant (IDA) is developed, verified, and discussed through an engineering design case.

Convolutional neural network and neural style transfer model are used to extract, reconstruct, and integrate the color features of style diagrams, and transfer them to product modeling to design new product images.

Article_Title: Product Innovation Design Method Based on BP Neural Network
Reference_Text_Preview: In this paper, a generative product design method, KENPI method, is proposed, which mainly includes ...
Full_Reference_Text: In this paper, a generative product design method, KENPI method, is proposed, which mainly includes mapping model construction, style transfer model construction, and transfer result analysis. BP neural network is used to construct the nonlinear mapping model of product modeling element space and product perceptual semantic space. The trained model is used to predict the product semantics in content maps and guide the selection of style maps. A generative innovative design method of KENPI products is proposed, which can automatically identify, extract, and reconstruct the content and color features of images, and transfer them to the product modeling in the content map to realize the real-time generation of new product images.

Full_Reference_Text: In this paper, a generative product design method, KENPI method, is proposed, which mainly includes mapping model construction, style transfer model construction, and transfer result analysis. The color features of images are reconstructed and integrated, and then transferred to product modeling, so as to generate new products with both content image modeling features and style image color features, which brings visual inspiration to users.
